mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly job growth (PAYEMS) indicates robust labor market conditions that boost consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days through increased corporate revenue expectations.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in high PAYEMS periods fall below 0.5% in > 40% of cases, or the correlation between PAYEMS and SPX returns drops below 0.15.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month decline in housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening demand for construction, which correlates with lower S&P 500 returns over 20 days due to reduced economic activity and corporate earnings expectations.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in HOUST decline periods exceed 1.2% in > 30% of cases, or the directional correlation between HOUST and SPX returns reverses sign.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in inflation (CPIAUCSL) and federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS) signal aggressive monetary tightening, which typically reduces equity valuations through higher discount rates and lower earnings growth prospects over 60 days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in co-moving CPIAUCSL/FEDFUNDS spikes exceed 2.5% in > 25% of cases, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes non-significant (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month rise in industrial production (INDPRO) indicates expanding manufacturing capacity, which drives S&P 500 returns over 20 days through improved corporate margins and economic growth expectations.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in INDPRO growth periods fall below 0.8% in > 35% of cases, or the correlation between INDPRO and SPX returns drops below 0.2.

mechanism_narrative: A 5-month decline in real sales (RSAFS) reflects weakening consumer demand, which predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 days due to reduced corporate revenue and earnings growth prospects.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in RSAFS decline periods exceed 1.8% in > 30% of cases, or the directional relationship between RSAFS and SPX returns reverses.

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month rise in unemployment (UNRATE) above 4.5% signals deteriorating labor market conditions, which predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days through reduced consumer spending and corporate earnings pressures.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in UNRATE spike periods exceed 1.0% in > 25% of cases, or the correlation between UNRATE and SPX returns becomes non-significant (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in GDP (GDPC1) and housing starts (HOUST) indicate weakening economic demand, which predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 days through reduced corporate earnings and risk-off sentiment.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 declines > 1% YoY and HOUST declines > 4% MoM
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in co-moving GDPC1/HOUST declines exceed 2.0% in > 20% of cases, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes non-significant (p > 0.1).
